topic: Retraction Tuning and Pressure/Linear Advance (LIN_ADVANCE) for the Hoverfly (Ender 3 Pro, Sprite Pro direct drive, SKR Mini E3 V2.0)
summary:
The Hoverfly has LIN_ADVANCE enabled but ADVANCE_K = 0.0, meaning the firmware is currently NOT applying any pressure-advance compensation. This is the single most impactful untuned gap on this machine: a direct-drive Sprite Pro should be running a small but non-zero K (community/Marlin guidance for direct-drive PLA clusters around 0.02-0.08, with the test sweep typically run 0 to 0.10 in 0.005-0.01 steps). Tuning K will sharpen corners, reduce bulging at the start of perimeters and gaps at the ends, and reduce ooze/stringing. Because K is a slicer/EEPROM setting (M900 K), it can be tuned and saved without reflashing - the ADVANCE_K 0.0 default just means it boots to "off."

Retraction: For a Sprite Pro direct-drive, the correct retraction window is short - Creality's own Sprite default is 0.6 mm at 85 mm/s, and the broader direct-drive consensus is 0.4-0.8 mm distance at 35-50 mm/s. The machine's slicer retraction (not in firmware - FWRETRACT is disabled, which is correct) should be tuned in this range; long Bowden-style retractions (3-6 mm) will cause grinding/heat-creep jams on a direct drive. Note the firmware's FWRETRACT block (RETRACT_LENGTH 3, RETRACT_FEEDRATE 45) is inactive because FWRETRACT is commented out, so those values do not affect prints - retraction is fully slicer-controlled. Tune K (pressure advance) FIRST, then retraction, because good pressure advance reduces the retraction distance needed.

Two firmware interactions to be aware of: (1) JUNCTION_DEVIATION is enabled (0.08) with CLASSIC_JERK off; LIN_ADVANCE still uses DEFAULT_EJERK (5.0) as the extruder jerk limit, and if LA's required extruder speed offset exceeds EJERK the planner silently lowers acceleration on that segment. (2) DEFAULT_EJERK is 5.0 (i.e. <10); Marlin's SanityCheck historically blocks EJERK<10 with LIN_ADVANCE unless ALLOW_LOW_EJERK is enabled. ALLOW_LOW_EJERK is currently commented out. EJERK 5.0 is a sensible value for a light direct-drive extruder, but the build may need ALLOW_LOW_EJERK enabled to compile/run cleanly with LA at that EJERK on some Marlin versions.
stlModel: Pressure-advance pattern test (Marlin pattern method, e.g. generated by OrcaSlicer's built-in PA Pattern test or Ellis' Pressure/Linear Advance generator) - a printed gcode pattern, not an STL. For retraction, a standard two-tower stringing test STL (e.g. retraction/stringing towers) or OrcaSlicer's built-in retraction test.
procedure:
PRESSURE / LINEAR ADVANCE (do this FIRST, before retraction):

PREREQUISITE: Confirm the extruder e-steps are calibrated (they are: E=423 for the Sprite) and flow/extrusion multiplier is roughly correct. LIN_ADVANCE is already enabled in firmware; ADVANCE_K just defaults to 0.0 (off).

METHOD A - OrcaSlicer Pattern test (recommended, easiest):
1. In OrcaSlicer set the printer's G-code flavor to Marlin (so the test emits M900 K... commands rather than Klipper SET_PRESSURE_ADVANCE).
2. Calibration menu -> Pressure Advance -> Pattern method. Choose the Direct Drive variant.
3. Recommended sweep for direct drive: Start PA 0.0, End PA 0.10, Step 0.005 (use 0.002 steps for a fine second pass once you've narrowed the range; Ellis notes direct drive benefits from 0.001-0.002 fine steps). These map directly to Marlin K values.
4. Print it. Read it: each band is printed at a higher K. Find the band/corner that is sharpest with the FEWEST artifacts - no bulge at the corner (K too low) and no gap/thinning/under-extrusion just before/after the corner (K too high). On the line-transition pattern, the ideal line is uniform width through the accel/decel zones.
5. Note the K value of the best band.

METHOD B - Marlin tower/manual M900:
- M900 K<value> sets it live; M900 K0 disables. Sweep 0 to 0.1. Print a tower or pattern, measure the best height, compute K = Start + Step x height.

PASS / FAIL:
- PASS: corners are crisp, perimeter start has no blob, perimeter end has no gap, line width uniform through speed changes. Expected best-K for direct-drive PLA: ~0.02-0.08 (Marlin v1.5 docs cite ~0.01-0.05 for direct drive; broader PLA range 0.1-2.0 includes Bowden - ignore the high end for this machine).
- FAIL / re-test: if you cannot get a corner that is simultaneously free of bulge AND gap at any K, suspect extruder mechanical issues (idler tension, gear backlash) rather than K.

SAVING THE VALUE:
- Easiest/portable: put M900 K<value> in the filament/material start g-code in OrcaSlicer (per-filament, survives nothing else needed).
- EEPROM: send M900 K<value> then M500 over a terminal (EEPROM_SETTINGS is enabled, so it persists across reboots). Verify with M503.
- Firmware default: optionally edit ADVANCE_K in Configuration_adv.h to the tuned value and reflash so the machine boots with it; EEPROM/M900 still overrides at print time.

RETRACTION (do AFTER pressure advance):
1. Set a baseline in OrcaSlicer (retraction is slicer-controlled; FWRETRACT is off in firmware, which is correct). Start at distance 0.6 mm, speed 40 mm/s, extra restart 0.0 - the Sprite/direct-drive baseline.
2. Run OrcaSlicer's Retraction test (temp/retraction tower) or print two thin spires ~50-60 mm apart and inspect the gap for strings.
3. Sweep distance 0.4 -> 0.8 mm. Pick the shortest distance that eliminates stringing without causing a blob/gap at travel-move starts.
4. Retraction speed: keep within 30-50 mm/s. If you see filament grinding or clicking, REDUCE speed (toward 30 mm/s) rather than increasing distance.
5. PASS: clean travel moves, no strings, no blobs on resume. FAIL: persistent stringing -> nudge distance up 0.1 mm and/or lower hotend temp 5C; grinding -> lower retraction speed.
6. Keep retraction distance SHORT - long retractions on a direct drive pull molten filament repeatedly past the heatbreak and cause heat-creep jams.

recommendations.title: Calibrate and set a non-zero ADVANCE_K (pressure advance) - currently 0.0 (off)
recommendations.currentValue: ADVANCE_K 0.0 (LIN_ADVANCE enabled but no compensation applied)
recommendations.recommendedValue: Tune per pattern test; expect ~0.02-0.08 for direct-drive PLA. Start the firmware default at ~0.04 once tuned.
recommendations.howToApply: Tune live: OrcaSlicer Calibration -> Pressure Advance -> Pattern (Direct Drive), G-code flavor = Marlin, sweep Start 0.0 / End 0.10 / Step 0.005. Then either (a) put M900 K<value> in filament start g-code, (b) send M900 K<value> then M500 to save to EEPROM, or (c) edit ADVANCE_K in Configuration_adv.h (line ~2119/2121) and reflash.
recommendations.rationale: LIN_ADVANCE is enabled but K=0 means zero pressure-advance compensation is being applied - the feature is effectively dormant. This is the biggest untuned gap for a direct-drive machine: a tuned K sharpens corners, removes start-of-perimeter blobs and end-of-perimeter gaps, and reduces ooze. K is a runtime/EEPROM value (M900) so it does not require reflashing to tune.

recommendations.title: Set slicer retraction to the direct-drive window (0.4-0.8 mm)
recommendations.currentValue: Slicer-controlled (FWRETRACT disabled in firmware - correct). No firmware retraction default applies.
recommendations.recommendedValue: Distance 0.6 mm (sweep 0.4-0.8), Speed 40 mm/s (range 35-50), extra restart 0.0
recommendations.howToApply: OrcaSlicer -> Printer/Filament settings -> Retraction: length 0.6 mm, speed 40 mm/s, deretraction 0/40. Tune via OrcaSlicer Retraction test or a stringing-tower print.
recommendations.rationale: The Sprite Pro is direct drive with a very short filament path; Creality's own Sprite default is 0.6 mm @ 85 mm/s and the direct-drive consensus is 0.4-0.8 mm. Long Bowden-style retractions (3-6 mm) cause grinding and heat-creep jams on a direct drive. Tune pressure advance first - it reduces the retraction distance needed.

recommendations.title: Keep retraction speed in 35-50 mm/s; reduce (not increase) on grinding
recommendations.howToApply: OrcaSlicer retraction speed = 40 mm/s. If filament clicks/grinds, drop toward 30 mm/s before increasing distance. Note DEFAULT_MAX_FEEDRATE E is 25 mm/s in firmware - this caps the actual E feedrate; raise E max-feedrate (M203 E50, or DEFAULT_MAX_FEEDRATE last value) if you want retraction speeds above 25 mm/s to actually be honored.
recommendations.rationale: Excessive retraction speed grinds/breaks filament; too slow leaves strings. Critically, the firmware E max feedrate of 25 mm/s will clamp any slicer retraction speed above 25 mm/s, so a 40-50 mm/s slicer setting is silently limited unless E max feedrate is raised.

recommendations.title: Enable ALLOW_LOW_EJERK to safely run LIN_ADVANCE with DEFAULT_EJERK 5.0
recommendations.howToApply: In Configuration_adv.h, uncomment #define ALLOW_LOW_EJERK and reflash.
recommendations.rationale: Marlin's SanityCheck historically blocks DEFAULT_EJERK < 10 when LIN_ADVANCE is enabled. ALLOW_LOW_EJERK exists specifically to permit low extruder jerk on direct-drive extruders (Prusa MK3, BMG, H2, Orbiter, etc.) with LA. EJERK 5.0 is appropriate for a light Sprite direct drive; enabling this flag avoids a compile/sanity error and documents the intent. Also note: when LA's required E speed offset exceeds EJERK, Marlin lowers print acceleration for that segment - keeping EJERK low is fine but is the lever if LA feels like it is killing acceleration.

recommendations.title: Tune pressure advance BEFORE retraction, then re-check retraction
recommendations.recommendedValue: Order: e-steps (done) -> flow -> pressure advance (K) -> retraction
recommendations.howToApply: Follow the procedure field: pattern-test K, save via M900/M500, then run the retraction/stringing test and pick the shortest distance that is string-free.
recommendations.rationale: Good pressure advance relieves nozzle pressure at the end of moves, which by itself reduces ooze and the retraction distance required. Tuning retraction first and K second would force re-tuning retraction afterward.

recommendations.title: Use a fine second-pass K sweep (0.002 steps) for direct drive
recommendations.recommendedValue: First pass 0 to 0.10 step 0.005; second pass narrowed range step 0.002
recommendations.howToApply: After the coarse pattern test narrows the best band, regenerate the OrcaSlicer/Ellis pattern around that value with 0.002 steps and re-print.
recommendations.rationale: Ellis specifically recommends re-running the test at 0.001-0.002 K intervals for direct-drive extruders because direct drive responds to very small K changes; the coarse pass alone leaves resolution on the table.

recommendations.title: Ignore the inactive FWRETRACT firmware block
recommendations.currentValue: FWRETRACT disabled; RETRACT_LENGTH 3, RETRACT_FEEDRATE 45 defined but inactive
recommendations.howToApply: No change. Be aware the RETRACT_LENGTH 3 mm value inside the FWRETRACT block is NOT in effect and is a Bowden-era default; do not copy it into the slicer.
recommendations.rationale: Because FWRETRACT is commented out, those values never run. Slicer-side retraction (G1 E moves) is the correct model for this machine. The 3 mm default would be wrong for a direct drive if it were ever enabled.

imageNeeds: Annotated photo of a printed pressure-advance pattern test showing too-low K (corner bulge), optimal K (sharp uniform corner), and too-high K (gap/under-extrusion before corner)
OrcaSlicer Pressure Advance Pattern calibration dialog showing Start/End/Step fields and the Direct Drive option with G-code flavor set to Marlin
Side-by-side stringing/retraction tower: under-retracted (heavy strings) vs tuned (clean) for a direct-drive Sprite
Diagram contrasting direct-drive short filament path (0.4-0.8 mm retraction) vs Bowden long path (3-6 mm) to explain why retraction distance is small
